if i have yellow teeth how long will it take to get pearly whites

The time it takes to go from yellow teeth to pearly whites can vary depending on various factors, such as the extent of discoloration, oral hygiene habits, lifestyle choices, and the methods used to whiten teeth. 

Professional dental treatments like whitening procedures performed by dentists can typically provide faster and more significant results. In-office whitening treatments may take only one or a few sessions, each lasting around 30-60 minutes. However, it is important to consult with a dental professional to determine the best approach based on your specific circumstances.

Over-the-counter whitening products, such as whitening toothpaste or strips, may take longer to show noticeable improvements, typically ranging from a few days to a few weeks depending on the product and individual response.

To maintain long-lasting results, it is important to practice good oral hygiene, avoid or limit certain stain-causing foods and beverages, and follow any recommendations or aftercare instructions provided by dental professionals or product manufacturers.

Which of the following would have the same effect on your checking account as writing a check?

 a
Using a credit card
 b
Using a debit card
 c
Making a deposit
 d
Taking out a loan
The correct answer is b) Using a debit card.

Which type of account would you suggest for someone who needs access to their money on a regular basis and is not concerned about earning a lot of interest?

 a
Money Market Account
 b
Checking Account
 c
Savings Account
 d
Certificate of Deposit

The correct answer is b) Checking Account. A checking account is ideal for someone who needs regular access to their money and is not primarily focused on earning interest. Checking accounts are designed for transactions such as deposits, withdrawals, bill payments, and everyday spending. They offer easy access to funds through checks, debit cards, and online banking, making them a convenient choice for managing day-to-day financial needs.

Whether you use a mobile app, ATM, or bank teller you must endorse the back of the check before you can deposit it.

 True
 False

False. Endorsing the back of a check is not always required to deposit it. Some banks may have specific policies regarding endorsements, but many banks allow customers to deposit checks without endorsement if they are depositing them through a mobile app, ATM, or bank teller. However, it's always a good practice to check with your bank to understand their specific requirements for check deposits.

Which of the following is not a feature of a debit card?

 a
Allows you to buy now, pay later
 b
Personal Identification number
 c
Draws directly from checking account
 d
Keeps an electronic record of spending

The correct answer is a) Allows you to buy now, pay later. Debit cards do not allow you to buy now and pay later like credit cards do. Debit cards draw funds directly from your checking account, often require a Personal Identification Number (PIN) for transactions, and typically keep an electronic record of your spending.

It is important to reconcile a bank statement so that ____.

 a
you can account for outstanding credits/deposits
 b
you can account for outstanding debits/withdrawals
 c
you don’t bounce a check
 d
all of the above

The correct answer is d) all of the above. Reconciling a bank statement is important for several reasons. By reconciling, you can ensure that your accounting records align with the bank's records, identify any discrepancies, and catch errors or fraudulent activity. It allows you to account for outstanding credits (deposits) and outstanding debits (withdrawals) that may not have cleared the bank yet. By doing so, you can also prevent overdrawing your account and bouncing a check. Therefore, all the given options are valid reasons for reconciling a bank statement.

Online banking, money market accounts, safe deposit boxes, and certificates of deposit are some of the services offered by many financial institutions.

 True
 False

True. Online banking, money market accounts, safe deposit boxes, and certificates of deposit are common services offered by many financial institutions. Online banking allows customers to access and manage their accounts electronically. Money market accounts provide a higher interest rate than regular savings accounts. Safe deposit boxes provide secure storage for valuable items. Certificates of deposit (CDs) are time deposits with fixed interest rates and maturity dates. These services can vary among different financial institutions, but they are commonly offered options.

A stop-payment order is a request that a bank or other financial institution not cash a particular check.
 True
 False

True. A stop-payment order is indeed a request made by a bank account holder to their bank or financial institution, instructing them not to cash or process a specific check. It is typically used when the account holder realizes that a check they issued will not be honored or needs to be canceled for various reasons, such as loss, error, or fraud. The purpose of a stop-payment order is to prevent the check from being cleared or paid by the bank.
